summary: Completed comprehensive testing of ELS MHD Step 02 - Incident Management. All 13 backend API tests passed (100%). Frontend UI tests passed for core functionality including incident list, filters, DEMO badges, Command Palette, and incident CRUD operations.

integration_issues.issue: Clicking 'Demo-Incident starten' button creates incident and activates it but does NOT navigate to /lage. The incident is created and shown in header, but user stays on incident list. This differs from the AppShell's handleStartDemo which does navigate.
After creating new incident via dialog on IncidentList page, user stays on incident list instead of navigating to /lage. The AppShell's NewIncidentDialog does navigate to /lage.

action_items: OPTIONAL: Consider making Demo-Incident button behavior consistent - either always navigate to /lage or never. Currently IncidentList stays on list while AppShell navigates.
OPTIONAL: Consider making NewIncidentDialog behavior consistent between IncidentList and AppShell usage.

success_rate.frontend: 95% (all core features working, minor navigation inconsistency)

seed_data_creation: Created and cleaned up TEST_Playwright_Incident for testing
context_for_next_testing_agent: Backend API fully tested with pytest. Frontend tested with Playwright for: incident list display, filter chips with counts, DEMO badges, Command Palette (Ctrl+K), incident CRUD (create, activate, close, reopen), localStorage persistence, header display with active incident. All data-testid attributes are properly implemented. Existing demo incidents in DB: MANV-Uebung Sued, Stadtfest 2026, Marathon Muenchen, Test Veranstaltung 2026.

backend: GET /api/meta returns version 0.2.0 and step info
POST /api/incidents/demo creates demo incident with demo:true, status:operativ
POST /api/incidents creates non-demo incident with start_at set
GET /api/incidents/{id} returns 200 for existing, 404 for unknown
PATCH /api/incidents/{id} with status=abgeschlossen sets end_at automatically
frontend: Filter chips (alle/operativ/geplant/abgeschlossen/demo) show counts and filter
Demo-Incident button creates incident and activates it
New Incident dialog opens and creates incident
Incident cards show DEMO badge only for demo=true
Header shows active incident name, DEMO badge (when demo), live duration
Header incident button navigates back to /
Command Palette opens via Ctrl+K with incident-switch entries
Active incident ID persisted in localStorage (els-active-incident)
Sidebar Lage is active when incident selected, disabled otherwise
